title: Steal Cloud Credentials via the Preview Fetcher
objective: Abuse the image-preview URL fetcher to reach the internal cloud metadata service and steal credentials.
briefing: The app has a preview feature at http://{ip}/api/preview?url=... that fetches any URL server-side and returns its content. There's no restriction on which addresses it's allowed to reach.
hints: The fetcher will request literally any URL you give it - including addresses that only the server itself could normally reach.
Cloud servers often expose their own instance credentials at a special internal-only address.
debrief: The preview feature fetches whatever URL is supplied, from the server itself - so pointing it at the cloud metadata address (169.254.169.254) made the server, which does have access to that internal-only endpoint, fetch and return its own IAM credentials to you. This is Server-Side Request Forgery (OWASP A10:2021): a server-side feature that fetches user-supplied URLs becomes a proxy an attacker can use to reach internal-only services the attacker could never address directly. The fix is an allowlist of permitted destinations/schemes, blocking requests to internal/link-local IP ranges, and using a metadata service version that requires a request-bound token (like IMDSv2) so a bare GET isn't enough even if SSRF occurs.

logs_append: [SSRF] preview endpoint used to reach internal metadata service - credentials exposed
